channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Why Trump's joint remarks to Congress won't be a 'State of the Union' address
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday night will stand at the front of the U.S. House chamber to address a joint session of Congress, the first of his second term in office.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A first-term Michigan senator is set to deliver the Democratic response to Trump's speech
item.description: Freshman Sen. Elissa Slotkin of Michigan will deliver the Democratic response to President Donald Trump’s first joint congressional address of his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico are now in effect, kicking off trade war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico finally went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, PAUL WISEMAN and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Census Bureau under Trump seeks permission to delete questions about gender identity
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau under the Trump administration has sought permission to delete questions about gender identity from a monthly survey that gathers near real-time data about American life.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: New Orleans to celebrate Mardi Gras Day with costumes and revelry despite weather concerns
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Thousands of revelers adorned in beads and outlandish costumes will fill the streets of New Orleans as the city celebrates Mardi Gras Day despite anticipated severe storms.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How South Carolina's execution of a condemned killer by firing squad will unfold
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When a South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat steps into the death row chamber Friday night, it won’t be lethal injection or electrocution that ends his life.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man who wore diamond watch to Lakers game sues arena, claims he was assaulted and robbed in restroom
item.description: A man who wore a diamond watch to watch the Los Angeles Lakers last year claims he was robbed in a restroom at their home arena in a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Second email to federal workers comes with caveat
item.description: On Friday, the Office of Personnel Management sent an email asking employees to list five bullets' worth of accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GOP rep introduces measure putting Trump face on $100 bill
item.description: First-term GOP Rep. Brandon Gill (Texas) has introduced a measure that would require all $100 bills to have President Trump’s face on them.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KTLA 5

item.title: Storms, possible twisters to threaten the South just as New Orleans celebrates Mardi Gras
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms with a threat of tornadoes are expected to punch through Louisiana and other parts of the South on Tuesday just as costumed revelers celebrate Mardi Gras with huge parades and partying in the streets of New Orleans and other cities in the region.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Woman stung by venomous critter while getting bag at Boston's Logan Airport
item.description: Massachusetts State Police said a 40-year-old woman retrieving her luggage at Boston Logan International Airport was stung by a scorpion on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Linda McMahon confirmed as Trump's Education secretary
item.description: Linda McMahon will be the next secretary of the Department of Education after passing a full Senate vote for her confirmation on Monday. The Senate voted 51-45 long party lines to confirm President Trump’s nominee to lead a federal agency he has been vocal about wanting to shut down.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Head of FBI New York office says he's retired from the bureau after being ordered to do so
item.description: The head of the FBI’s New York field office, who was reported to have resisted Justice Department efforts to scrutinize agents who participated in politically sensitive investigations, has told coworkers that he has retired from the bureau after being directed to do so.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Human cannonball injured at Southern California county fair
item.description: Chachi "The Rocketman" Valencia was transported to a local hospital after he had fallen out of a safety net during the performance.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4

item.title: How did Americans view the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting? Poll shows stark divide
item.description: About half of those polled said neither Ukraine nor the U.S. walked away as a winner from last week's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KRON4

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New charge filed against son of Buc-ee's co-founder
item.description: Mitchell Wasek, 29, is being charged with a third-degree felony offense of possession or promotion of child pornography, with a $50,000 bond.
item.dc:creator: Dalton Huey, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio signals Zelenskyy now willing to sign Trump's minerals deal and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daryl Hannah voices support for Ukraine on Oscars stage
item.description: The 64-year-old performer's brief remark came days after an explosive confrontation between President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Photos: The 2025 Oscars Red Carpet in Los Angeles
item.description: Hollywood's biggest stars and most talented artists are set to be honored Sunday evening during the 97th Academy Awards held at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood. But before the awards ceremony itself begins, the stars are walking the iconic Red Carpet where fashion takes center stage.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: Rubio says Trump would be 'on his way to the Nobel Peace Prize' if he were a Democrat
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio fiercely defended President Trump’s approach to negotiating a peace deal between Ukraine and Russia on Sunday, saying the media public would be praising his efforts if he were a Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Joe Highsmith goes from making the cut to a PGA Tour winner at the Cognizant Classic
item.description: PALM BEACH GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Joe Highsmith could feel himself crumbling under the pressure at the Cognizant Classic. This was on Friday, when the only thing at stake was making the cut.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Grocery shoppers are using these strategies to combat inflation: survey
item.description: For the vast majority of Americans who don't grow what they eat, it's not feasible to avoid eye-watering prices at the grocery store. However, a recent study found that most shoppers are getting creative to save money.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Anti-DOGE protests at Tesla stores target Elon Musk's bottom line
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Demonstrators gathered outside Tesla stores across the U.S. Saturday to protest the automaker's billionaire CEO, Elon Musk, and his push to slash government spending on behalf of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: RODRIQUE NGOWI and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee could add 'covenant marriage' with proposed bill
item.description: One Tennessee lawmaker would like to add a new kind of marriage to the books through a bill filed in the General Assembly.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, Fox 5

item.title: Firefighters working to contain wildfires in North, South Carolina
item.description: Crews have been battling wildfires in North and South Carolina this weekend, competing with dry conditions and gusty winds.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Enabling this phone setting reduces anxiety, Gen Z says
item.description: It can create "a buffer against unwanted communications and potential social pressures," a psychotherapist said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Connor Zilisch's 'not smart' moves lead him to victory at Focused Health 250 in Austin
item.description: Zilisch shrugged off early and late trouble to capture the checkered flag in the NASCAR Xfinity Series Focused Health 250 on Saturday at Circuit of the Americas. It's his second career win on NASCAR's second circuit.
item.dc:creator: Grace Reader, WFLA, NBC

item.title: David Johansen, singer from the seminal punk band the New York Dolls, dies at 75
item.description: Johansen died Friday at his home in New York City, Jeff Kilgour, a family spokesperson told The Associated Press. It was revealed in early 2025 that he had stage 4 cancer and a brain tumor.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Fox 5

item.title: Barbie celebrates the world of music with 2025 Career of the Year, Alicia Keys doll
item.description: Barbie is celebrating all things music this year, announcing its 2025 Career of the Year and a new doll, highlighting a popular musical artist.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jury finds Illinois landlord guilty of murder, hate crime in 2023 attack on Palestinian American boy
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — A jury found an Illinois landlord guilty of murder and hate crime charges Friday for the brutal killing of a 6-year-old whose mother rented rooms in the man’s home, an attack that spiked fears over anti-Muslim discrimination in the earliest days of the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and MELISSA PEREZ WINDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9
